clinical_coherence.notes: 72F South Asian retired professor with early backward falls (18 months, 8 falls), vertical supranuclear gaze palsy (downgaze>upgaze), preserved oculocephalic reflexes, axial-predominant rigidity with retrocollis, erect posture, spastic dysarthria, dysphagia, personality changes (irritability, reduced empathy), poor levodopa response. Classic PSP-Richardson syndrome. Exam is meticulously detailed with procerus sign, applause sign, motor impersistence.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI shows hummingbird sign and morning glory sign with reduced midbrain area (0.85 cm2) and midbrain-to-pons ratio (0.47). EEG shows bifrontal theta (nonspecific). DaTscan symmetric bilateral (atypical for PD). Levodopa challenge shows only 11.1% improvement. Neuropsych reveals frontal-executive profile with preserved memory and visuospatial function. Swallowing study shows silent aspiration. Tau PET shows subcortical 4R tauopathy pattern. All perfectly concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G23.1 (PSP) correct. Differential includes PD, CBD, MSA, DLB with appropriate reasoning. Critical actions correctly prioritize eye movement assessment, documenting poor levodopa response, midbrain MRI, swallowing assessment, and fall prevention. Contraindicated actions include DBS and escalating levodopa indefinitely.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Excellent puzzle. Patient referred as 'atypical PD' on levodopa. Red herrings include initial PD diagnosis, positive DaTscan, and levodopa on medication list. Requires recognition of vertical gaze palsy, early falls, and characteristic imaging to diagnose PSP.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 72F with HTN, osteoporosis, hypothyroidism. Realistic for PSP onset. Female sex adds diversity (PSP slightly more common in males but occurs in females).

red_herring_quality.notes: Three well-designed red herrings: initial PD diagnosis by PCP, positive DaTscan (abnormal in PSP too), and carbidopa-levodopa on medication list implying PD was correct. All are realistic and properly addressed.

educational_value.notes: Exceptional teaching case. Covers PSP-Richardson cardinal features (vertical gaze palsy, early falls, axial rigidity, retrocollis, poor levodopa response), oculocephalic reflex to confirm supranuclear localization, hummingbird/morning glory signs, frontal-subcortical cognitive profile, silent aspiration risk, and tau PET. Outstanding discriminative case.

strengths: Comprehensive multimodal workup including tau PET
Silent aspiration on swallowing study highlights mortality risk
Detailed eye movement examination with oculocephalic reflex confirmation
